title: Marbella Fishing Charter Half-Day Price: Guide & Costs 2026
topic: fishing charter half day price in Marbella
html:
A half-day fishing charter in Marbella typically costs between €350 and €650, depending on the boat size and crew. Departures from Puerto Banús or the Real Club de Pescadores offer direct access to prime Mediterranean grounds. BoatHire24 connects you with verified skippers across the Costa del Sol.

How much does a half-day fishing charter cost in Marbella?

Half-day fishing charters in Marbella cost from €350 to €650 for up to 6 guests. The price covers a 4-hour trip with gear, bait, and a skipper. Shared charters can be as low as €80 per person if you join a group. You can hire a boat in a few minutes.

To compare verified listings and check availability, you can hire a boat through BoatHire24. Many charters include soft drinks and light snacks. One option worth a look is the Sunseeker 63 for hire.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most fishing charters depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina west of Marbella town. Other common launch points include the Real Club de Pescadores in the old port, Cabopino Marina near Cabopino, and the small harbour at San Pedro de Alcántara. From these marinas, boats often head towards the scenic Cabo de Plata or the rocky reefs off Salobreña. The Gibraltar Strait, about 40 nautical miles south, is a productive zone for larger game fish.

Which boat to rent for a fishing charter

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 63), Ideal for a luxury half-day trip, accommodating 6 to 8 guests, from €550
  2. Sport fisherman (e.g. Astondoa 55), Purpose-built for angling with fighting chairs and outriggers, for 4 to 6 guests, from €450
  3. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), Fast and agile for light tackle fishing, up to 4 guests, from €350
  4. Catamaran, Stable platform for family fishing, up to 8 guests, from €500 for a half-day
  5. Sailing yacht, Silent trolling under sail, ideal for groups of 4, from €400

For a premium experience, consider the Sunseeker 63 for hire; it offers comfort and speed to reach distant fishing grounds quickly. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

Cost breakdown for fishing charters in Marbella

  • Shared half-day charter: from €80 per person (minimum 4 guests)
  • Private small motorboat half-day: €350 for up to 4 guests
  • Catamaran full-day: €800 for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed yacht half-day: €650 for up to 6 guests, including lunch

Season and licence requirements

The best season for fishing in Marbella runs from May to September, when the Levante wind (easterly) is less frequent and sea temperatures are warm. For a charter, you do not need a personal licence because the skipper holds all necessary permits. The operator will confirm exact requirements, but generally, for bareboat rental in Spain you need the Spanish recreational licence (Licencia de Navegación) or an equivalent ICC. To see how booking works, visit the booking FAQ.

If you're self-sufficient and want to fish from a private boat, remember to register your catch with the local cofradía (fishermen's guild) in Puerto Banús or Marbella old port.

How does booking a fishing charter work?

After choosing your preferred charter, you can book directly through the platform. Payment is handled securely, and you receive instant confirmation. For details on cancellation and deposit policies, refer to the booking FAQ on BoatHire24.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 to €650 per person or boat
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half-day) or 8 hours (full-day)
  • Guest capacity: 4 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Do you need a licence to rent a fishing boat in Marbella?

If you book a crewed charter, you do not need a licence; the skipper will handle navigation and fishing licences.

What species can you catch on a Marbella fishing charter?

Common catches include dentex, sea bass, amberjack, and occasionally bluefin tuna in the summer months.

Can you keep the fish you catch?

Yes, the captain will help you prepare the catch, but you must follow local size and bag limits.

What if the weather is bad on the day of the trip?

The skipper will reschedule or offer a full refund if conditions are unsafe.
